jokes.text: All I want is to enjoy the quiet desolation of space
jokes.setup: Fry wants peaceful quiet for his birthday
jokes.punchline: Immediately interrupted by surprise party
jokes.explanation: Classic setup/payoff with good character specificity - Fry's desire for space solitude fits his character

jokes.text: Only a few. Less than 40.
jokes.setup: Fry asks how many people were invited
jokes.punchline: 40 people is considered 'only a few'
jokes.explanation: Simple absurdist math humor - 40 being 'a few' is genuinely funny misdirection

jokes.text: Chicken cordon bleu!? B'kaw!
jokes.setup: Someone mentions Fry looks 'blue'
jokes.punchline: Mishears as chicken dish and makes chicken sound
jokes.explanation: Clever wordplay combined with physical comedy (chicken sound) - unexpectedly sophisticated

jokes.text: We got a code 99. Birthday boy unresponsive.
jokes.setup: Emergency-style announcement
jokes.punchline: It's just about Fry not enjoying his party
jokes.explanation: Good escalation of party desperation to military-style emergency language

jokes.text: Emergency! All butts to battle cushions!
jokes.setup: Ship emergency requiring battle stations
jokes.punchline: Battle stations become 'battle cushions' (seats)
jokes.explanation: Excellent wordplay that maintains urgency while being absurd - very quotable

jokes.text: Scruffy begs to differ. I see it as more of a gray-brown, or perhaps toasted umber.
jokes.setup: Black hole emergency
jokes.punchline: Scruffy calmly corrects the color description with paint swatches
jokes.explanation: Perfect Scruffy character moment - his janitor expertise applied to cosmic horror with deadpan delivery

jokes.text: We heard a girlish scream. Are you okay?
jokes.setup: Someone screamed
jokes.punchline: Described as 'girlish' - implies it was from a male character
jokes.explanation: Fairly basic masculine/feminine humor - functional but not particularly clever

jokes.text: I was busy making a tourniquet out of my tiny, tiny shirt.
jokes.setup: Character was busy during emergency
jokes.punchline: Making medical equipment from comically small clothing
jokes.explanation: Good visual comedy - the repetition of 'tiny' emphasizes the absurdity

jokes.text: Like Fire Island after Labor Day.
jokes.setup: Describing a barren wasteland
jokes.punchline: Compared to Fire Island post-season
jokes.explanation: Clever cultural reference - Fire Island being a gay resort town that empties after summer

jokes.text: That's the tasteless suburban teardown I grew up in!
jokes.setup: Fry criticizes the house as tasteless
jokes.punchline: Realizes it's his childhood home
jokes.explanation: Good character revelation - Fry inadvertently insulting his own upbringing

jokes.text: Not everything in life is about winning. But most things are. Almost everything.
jokes.setup: Dad tries to comfort Fry about not being good at games
jokes.punchline: Immediately undermines his own advice by escalating
jokes.explanation: Excellent escalation structure - starts reassuring then completely contradicts itself in a relatable parental way

jokes.text: Dr. Peeper
jokes.setup: Fry remembers details from his party
jokes.punchline: Knockoff Dr. Pepper brand name
jokes.explanation: Simple brand parody - efficient but not particularly clever

jokes.text: It was we! - He meant us. It was us! - I don't know. I thought 'we' sounded better.
jokes.setup: Alien reveals they recreated the house
jokes.punchline: Debate over proper grammar usage
jokes.explanation: Smart grammar humor - aliens being pedantic about objective vs. subjective case

jokes.text: I'ds likes to hears about the grammars.
jokes.setup: Grammar discussion happening
jokes.punchline: Character interested in grammar while using terrible grammar
jokes.explanation: Excellent ironic contrast - wanting to learn about grammar while demonstrating complete ignorance of it

jokes.text: Barrel of Primates!
jokes.setup: Introducing a classic children's game
jokes.punchline: Copyright-free version of 'Barrel of Monkeys'
jokes.explanation: Simple brand parody - functional joke about copyright avoidance

jokes.text: They call me Monkey Breath for a reason.
jokes.setup: Character expresses enthusiasm for monkey game
jokes.punchline: Has embarrassing monkey-related nickname
jokes.explanation: Character-specific humor that implies backstory - decent setup for the game

jokes.text: I'm a leg. Every day is leg day.
jokes.setup: Character compliments another's strength
jokes.punchline: Explains they're literally a leg in the human chain
jokes.explanation: Brilliant gym culture reference applied to literal human chain - perfect economy and unexpected insight

jokes.text: I'm a leg. Every day is leg day.
jokes.setup: Someone comments they must work out a lot while in barrel
jokes.punchline: Character is literally acting as a leg, so every day is leg day
jokes.explanation: Clever literal interpretation of workout terminology in the absurd context

jokes.text: Did we mention there's a twist? I knew you were twist guys!
jokes.setup: Aliens about to reveal game twist
jokes.punchline: Character immediately recognizes their pattern
jokes.explanation: Good meta-humor about the aliens' predictable pattern of adding twists

jokes.text: It's actually a highly alkaline liquid. That's the twist! It'll dissolve you twice as fast as acid!
jokes.setup: Aliens say it's not acid to be reassuring
jokes.punchline: Alkaline is actually worse than acid
jokes.explanation: Excellent subversion - seems like relief then becomes worse. Good science-based humor

jokes.text: But I have a fear of dissolving!
jokes.setup: Learning about the alkaline death pit
jokes.punchline: Has very specific phobia about dissolving
jokes.explanation: Good absurdist specificity - who has a fear specifically of dissolving?

jokes.text: Phil's the sentimental favorite, but the smart money's on four-eyes.
jokes.setup: Aliens commenting on the death game like sports announcers
jokes.punchline: Treating lethal children's games as sporting events with betting
jokes.explanation: Good tonal contrast - casual sports commentary on life-or-death situation

jokes.text: The name's Gedgie, and it may look close, but I've got the Gedge.
jokes.setup: Gedgie talking trash during the game
jokes.punchline: Uses his name as a verb meaning 'advantage'
jokes.explanation: Creative character-specific wordplay - turning his name into competitive slang

jokes.text: I hope my grandma's proud of me down in Hell.
jokes.setup: Fry wins his first game ever
jokes.punchline: Casually mentions grandmother is in Hell
jokes.explanation: Brilliantly dark and unexpected - casual family reference with shocking theological implication

jokes.text: Nice job, Phil, but I'll Gedge you next time. That never gets old.
jokes.setup: Gedgie using his name-based catchphrase again
jokes.punchline: Claims his own joke never gets old
jokes.explanation: Good character consistency - annoying kid who thinks his own jokes are funnier than they are

jokes.text: As for me, I don't have any motivations.
jokes.setup: Aliens explaining their motivations for the death games
jokes.punchline: One alien has literally zero motivation
jokes.explanation: Perfect deadpan absurdism - complete lack of motivation delivered matter-of-factly

jokes.text: And I have two motivations! Gambling, and making these cool masks.
jokes.setup: Following the unmotivated alien
jokes.punchline: This one has very specific, mundane motivations for cosmic horror
jokes.explanation: Good contrast with previous alien - banal hobbies as motivation for death games

jokes.text: It just says, 'Fun for ages 3 to 99.' - No wonder I'm not enjoying it!
jokes.setup: Reading the rules, finds age restriction
jokes.punchline: Professor is over 99 so he's outside the fun age range
jokes.explanation: Excellent character-specific humor - Professor's extreme age creating unexpected game restriction

jokes.text: Damn! My dribbling and differentiating arm!
jokes.setup: Professor injures his arm in the game
jokes.punchline: Describes it in terms of his dual basketball/math expertise
jokes.explanation: Perfect Professor character moment - combining sports and advanced mathematics in injury complaint

jokes.text: Is okay. Is only water. Just as I foresaw. Wait.
jokes.setup: Zoidberg confidently claims he predicted the outcome
jokes.punchline: Immediately realizes he was wrong
jokes.explanation: Classic Zoidberg moment - false confidence immediately deflated

jokes.text: My parents and my grandmother a-are dead!
jokes.setup: Celebrating victory with birthday cake
jokes.punchline: Leela's trauma response to the fake family
jokes.explanation: Good dark humor - Leela's genuine trauma contrasted with fake celebration

jokes.text: I'm so sorry for your loss, Leela. Can I have their cake?
jokes.setup: Leela's emotional breakdown about dead family
jokes.punchline: Someone immediately asks for their cake portions
jokes.explanation: Brilliantly callous timing - perfect dark comedy juxtaposition of grief and opportunism

jokes.text: I'm so sorry for your loss, Leela. Can I have their cake?
jokes.setup: Leela's emotional outburst about dead family
jokes.punchline: Fry's inappropriate but practical response about cake
jokes.explanation: Perfect Fry moment showing his social obliviousness and food obsession

jokes.text: That's a lot of candles, old man. You'll never do it.
jokes.setup: Fry needs to blow out many candles to save everyone
jokes.punchline: Kid trash-talks him before life-or-death breath test
jokes.explanation: Good character consistency - annoying competitive kid even during death games

jokes.text: He's still blowing somehow!
jokes.setup: Fry attempting impossible candle-blowing task
jokes.punchline: Continues blowing impossibly long
jokes.explanation: Basic physical comedy - cartoonish lung capacity

jokes.text: My only regret is not getting any cake!
jokes.setup: Character dying from failed candle challenge
jokes.punchline: Final regret is about missing dessert, not dying
jokes.explanation: Good dark humor about misplaced priorities - cake more important than life

jokes.text: Nobody panic! Two to a seat! You heard her! Start panicking!
jokes.setup: Leela tells everyone not to panic during musical chairs
jokes.punchline: Bender immediately tells them to start panicking
jokes.explanation: Perfect Bender character moment - immediately contradicts leadership with chaos

jokes.text: It'd take all three Hulks to break that. Incredible, She, and Hogan.
jokes.setup: Commenting on the strength needed to break piñata
jokes.punchline: Lists Incredible Hulk, She-Hulk, and Hulk Hogan as 'three Hulks'
jokes.explanation: Brilliant wordplay connecting comic book characters with wrestler through shared 'Hulk' name

jokes.text: Since no one likes candy, we have filled our piñata with quids!
jokes.setup: Explaining piñata contents
jokes.punchline: Contains gambling currency instead of candy
jokes.explanation: Decent subversion of expectations - money instead of candy fits alien gambling theme

jokes.text: I like candy. My husband is dead!
jokes.setup: Aliens claim no one likes candy
jokes.punchline: Character protests loving candy then mentions dead husband
jokes.explanation: Awkward combination of minor complaint with major tragedy - decent dark humor

jokes.text: Finally, I can give up limbo and start a professional sex league.
jokes.setup: Hermes celebrates winning money from piñata
jokes.punchline: Plans to quit limbo for professional sex league
jokes.explanation: Brilliantly absurd career transition - from limbo dancing to sex league management

jokes.text: Penultimate means next to last. You lose the vocabulary game!
jokes.setup: Fry thinks 'penultimate' means final
jokes.punchline: Aliens correct him and declare vocabulary game loss
jokes.explanation: Good educational humor - common vocabulary mistake turned into game mechanic

jokes.text: It's not magic, for God's sake.
jokes.setup: Explaining their reality-warping machine's limitations
jokes.punchline: Drawing arbitrary line at baseball creation while doing impossible things
jokes.explanation: Excellent ironic understatement - beings with god-like powers claiming limits aren't magic

jokes.text: Just last night, we had bicentennial meatloaf. Red, white, and green.
jokes.setup: Explaining things get lost in fridge
jokes.punchline: 200-year-old meatloaf with wrong patriotic colors
jokes.explanation: Decent gross-out humor about extremely old food with wrong flag colors (green instead of blue)

jokes.text: The woman you occasionally say you love.
jokes.setup: Leela trying to convince Fry to help her
jokes.punchline: Describes herself as woman he only 'occasionally' loves
jokes.explanation: Good relationship humor - Leela's realistic assessment of Fry's inconsistent romantic declarations

jokes.text: I always love you, Leela, but there are two things I just can't do. Almost anything, and cheat.
jokes.setup: Fry explaining his limitations
jokes.punchline: Lists incompetence alongside moral principle as equals
jokes.explanation: Perfect Fry character moment - self-aware incompetence delivered with touching sincerity

jokes.text: You're not the winners. Who said you were the winners? I forgot to mention the winners are the ones who don't find the baseballs! That's on me, guys.
jokes.setup: Everyone who found baseballs thinks they won
jokes.punchline: Rules were backwards all along, alien casually admits oversight
jokes.explanation: Good rule reversal with casual alien accountability for the confusion

jokes.text: That was some impressive reverse cheating, Fry.
jokes.setup: Fry helped others find baseballs despite wanting to win
jokes.punchline: Describes helping as 'reverse cheating'
jokes.explanation: Clever term for altruism - helping opponents as opposite of cheating

jokes.text: It is the donkey's Achilles butt. All its vital organs are clustered there. Just like me!
jokes.setup: Explaining donkey's vulnerable spot
jokes.punchline: Zoidberg relates to having all organs in butt area
jokes.explanation: Good anatomy humor - 'Achilles butt' pun plus Zoidberg's alien physiology

jokes.text: How's this for cheating?
jokes.setup: Fry finally decides to break his no-cheating principle
jokes.punchline: Dramatically announces his first cheat
jokes.explanation: Good character moment - Fry's moral compromise delivered with dramatic flair

jokes.text: Everything's coming up Gedgie. Beat that, REO Cheatwagon.
jokes.setup: Gedgie confident about winning final game
jokes.punchline: Combines band name REO Speedwagon with cheating accusation
jokes.explanation: Creative insult combining 80s band with cheating theme, good character voice

jokes.text: You stabbed that donkey like a professional donkey stabber!
jokes.setup: Dad praising Fry's success in the game
jokes.punchline: Compares him to professional in very specific violent occupation
jokes.explanation: Brilliantly absurd profession - implies professional donkey stabbing is a career path

jokes.text: Happy birthday, Fry. I hope you had fun cheating 'cause nobody else did!
jokes.setup: Gedgie bitter about losing to Fry
jokes.punchline: Ruins birthday mood with accusation and collective blame
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing childhood resentment and poor sportsmanship

jokes.text: My vital ass organs!
jokes.setup: Donkey gets stabbed in its vulnerable area
jokes.punchline: Confirms all vital organs really are in its butt
jokes.explanation: Good callback - confirms the absurd anatomy explanation was accurate

jokes.text: My vital ass organs! You are a cheater!
jokes.setup: Donkey gets stabbed in vulnerable area
jokes.punchline: References earlier 'vital organs clustered' joke while accusing cheating
jokes.explanation: Good callback to setup joke about organ placement

jokes.text: Did I not mention that either?
jokes.setup: Reveals they can bring people back to life
jokes.punchline: Casually admits to forgetting another crucial detail
jokes.explanation: Good running gag - aliens consistently forgetting to mention crucial information

jokes.text: The machine can easily bring people back to life. Did I not mention that either?
jokes.setup: Everyone thought death was permanent throughout episode
jokes.punchline: Alien casually mentions resurrection capability as oversight
jokes.explanation: Major revelation delivered with perfect casual alien obliviousness

jokes.text: He went down like a sack of shoes.
jokes.setup: Commenting on how easily Fry was defeated
jokes.punchline: Uses unusual simile - 'sack of shoes' instead of potatoes
jokes.explanation: Decent variation on common phrase - shoes instead of potatoes/bricks is mildly amusing

jokes.text: Yeah, I know. He still can't work a zipper.
jokes.setup: Discussing Fry not being competitive
jokes.punchline: Cites inability to use basic clothing fastener
jokes.explanation: Good character consistency - specific example of Fry's fundamental life incompetence
